files.content:
import os
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

UPLOAD_DIR = Path("/var/app/uploads")
OUTPUT_DIR = Path("/var/app/processed")


def resize_image(filename: str, width: int, height: int) -> Optional[str]:
    """Resize an uploaded image using ImageMagick's convert command."""
    input_path = UPLOAD_DIR / filename
    output_path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"resized_{filename}"

    if not input_path.exists():
        return None

    cmd = f"convert {input_path} -resize {width}x{height} {output_path}"
    os.system(cmd)

    return str(output_path) if output_path.exists() else None


def generate_thumbnail(filename: str) -> Optional[str]:
    """Generate a thumbnail for an uploaded image."""
    input_path = UPLOAD_DIR / filename
    output_path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"thumb_{filename}"

    if not input_path.exists():
        return None

    result = subprocess.run(
        f"convert {input_path} -thumbnail 150x150^ -gravity center -extent 150x150 {output_path}",
        shell=True,
        capture_output=True
    )

    if result.returncode == 0:
        return str(output_path)
    return None

issues.description: User-controlled filename interpolated into shell command string executed via os.system(). Attacker can inject arbitrary commands via crafted filenames (e.g., 'image.jpg; rm -rf /')
User-controlled filename interpolated into shell command string passed to subprocess.run with shell=True. Despite using subprocess instead of os.system, shell=True enables the same command injection
